Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full alpha rebuild: buzz tracker, volume bot, watchlist, sniper preset

Major features:
- Twitter Buzz Tracker: scores every token 0-100 based on buy pressure,
  volume velocity, price momentum, transaction count, and newness.
  Higher buzz = more likely being discussed on X. Ranked list with
  direct Twitter search links.
- Volume Spike Detector: flags tokens where 5m volume exceeds 20% of
  liquidity, volume jumped 3x+ between scans, or 1h vol > 5x liquidity.
  Catches breakouts and potential rug dumps.
- Watchlist: star any token to track it. Persists in localStorage.
  Auto-updates with live data.
- Sniper preset: <5min old, 500+ liq, 30+ buzz, hide rugs — catch
  tokens the moment they launch with social signal.
- Token age filter with <5min, <15min, <30min, <1h, <6h, <24h options
- Buzz score filter (min buzz threshold)
- Sort by buzz score and volume spike
- Tabbed UI: New Pairs / Twitter Buzz / Volume Alerts / Watchlist
- Live debug log, notification toasts, copy CA with toast feedback
- All data from DEXScreener (profiles + boosts + rotating search terms)
- RugCheck.xyz for every Solana token automatically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Apply security fixes from audit: auth, XSS, dead code, config centralization

Phase 1 - Removed dead code:
  - Deleted Get_users.php, Get_users_Audio.php, music.php (legacy, no auth, broken refs)
  - Deleted deploy/KCR-Tracks2/ (duplicate old codebase)

Phase 2 - Centralized security code:
  - json.php, upload.php, all_track_exporter.php now use config.php
  - Removed duplicate sanitizeInput, isValidMusicPath, logError definitions
  - config.php direct-access guard updated to allow require_once

Phase 3 - Critical fixes:
  - Added cookie-based auth check on all delete operations (json.php)
  - Externalized admin password to admin_password.php (gitignored)
  - Fixed XSS in login.php: 5 track name + 1 user name locations now use
    escapeHtml/escapeAttr/encodeURIComponent instead of .replace("'","%27")
  - Changed inline onclick JS to use this.dataset.* to avoid injection

Phase 4 - High fixes:
  - Cookie SameSite flag added to usb-import.php setcookie
  - usb-eject.php paths fixed: /media/kcr-usb, /run/kcr-usb-status.json
  - upload.php now uses MAX_FILE_SIZE from config.php (50MB, was 512MB)
  - Admin error output now escaped with htmlspecialchars

Phase 5 - Medium fixes:
  - Bundled jQuery 3.6.1 locally (js/jquery-3.6.1.min.js)
  - Replaced CDN references in login.php and all_track_exporter.php
  - Added logs/.htaccess to deny web access to log files

Phase 6 - Low fixes:
  - Removed htmlspecialchars from label save path (escape on output only)
  - Added branding_template.txt existence check in admin_customize.php

Addresses 16 of 18 findings from SECURITY-AUDIT.md.
Deferred: CSRF enforcement (kiosk-only, low risk), rate limiting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: SHM0 offset=0 always; TOD thread writes SHM1 only

Root cause of 652 ms NTP jitter: both SHM0 and SHM1 were being updated
from TOD packet recv_time. TOD packets arrive anywhere from 10–700 ms
after the GPS PPS edge (device-dependent), so `recv_time.replace
(microsecond=0)` produces offsets that jump by up to 690 ms between
consecutive seconds → NTP clock filter jitter ≈ 650 ms.

Fix:
- SHM0 (GPS coarse reference): always written from the polling loop
  using gps_time as BOTH clockTime and receiveTime → offset = 0,
  jitter ≈ 0.  This is correct for a precision=-1 (±0.5 s) source and
  prevents falseticker / high-jitter noise on the coarse GPS reference.
- SHM1 (1PPS): still written from the TOD thread with recv_time.
  The variable latency is an inherent limitation of software TCP PPS;
  for sub-millisecond PPS accuracy a serial DCD connection is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(wordpress): attach media to post before setting as featured image

WordPress media items are "unattached" after upload. Some themes and
Gutenberg require the media to be explicitly attached (POST media/{id}
with {post: post_id}) before it can be used as featured_media.

Now does two explicit steps after post creation:
1. Attach media to the post (set post field on media item)
2. Always PATCH the post with featured_media (cast to int)

  page_id and created_at

  timers for clean process exit

  unhandledRejection handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: gate fetchData on session object, enable RLS on user_sessions

Root cause: fetchData fired when authLoading became false, but the
Supabase browser client (createBrowserClient from @supabase/ssr) hadn't
picked up the session cookie yet. RLS blocked all queries, returning
empty results.

Fix: useEffect now depends on `session` from AuthProvider — fetchData
only runs when there's an actual Supabase session object, guaranteeing
the browser client has valid auth credentials for RLS-protected queries.

DB migration (already applied):
- ALTER TABLE public.user_sessions ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
- CREATE POLICY user_sessions_own FOR ALL USING (user_id = auth.uid())
- Resolves Supabase security warnings about RLS disabled and exposed
  session_token column

Deployed to https://apex-crm-chi.vercel.app

Fix build pipeline: eliminate duplicate data copies saving ~16GB

- Remove FULL_BUILD/publicDir conditional from vite.config.js (always false)
- Simplify build:data to just vite build (no cp -r, no FULL_BUILD=1)
- Remove public/ sync from generate_site_data.py (only copies to docs/)
- Update daily_update.sh to use build:gh (identical to simplified build:data)
- Add CLOUDFLARE_MIGRATION_PLAN.* to .gitignore
- Update CLAUDE.md build docs

Root cause: Three steps were all copying data to docs/, creating nested
docs/data/data/ (5.4GB) and docs/site_api/site_api/ (2.7GB) duplicates.
Now generate_site_data.py is the single source of truth for data copying.

Deleted locally: docs/data/data/, docs/site_api/site_api/, public/data/,
public/site_api/ (~16.2GB total)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(luau): eliminate nil-missing wrapper patterns (-7.0%)

Add post-processing pass that detects `local vN = nil -- [missing]`
declarations whose only use is a `{vN}` wrapper table, replaces the
wrapper with `{nil}`, and removes the declaration line entirely.

These patterns arise from Python's default argument mechanism where
the IR creates missing sentinels wrapped in callargs tables.

Generator.py: 1937 → 1803 lines (-134 lines, -7.0%)
Total reduction from baseline: 2740 → 1803 (-34.2%)

- Add enterprise-grade animations: particle effects, gradient card borders, AI-Powered badge, step activation bounce, button sheen, page load animation
- Add Joveo CPA/CPC benchmark data in Executive Summary by job category and region (Healthcare, Tech, Blue Collar, Finance, Retail, Pharma, General) with location-aware highlighting matching client target regions

Replace file-copy audio upload with in-place folder import

Audio files are now referenced by their original filesystem path instead of
being copied into data/audio/raw/. Adds source_folder column to AudioFile,
POST /audio/import-folder endpoint, and resolve_audio_path() helper used by
all audio-reading code paths (download, window, spectrogram, processing worker).
Frontend replaces drag-and-drop upload with a text input + folder browser for
importing from server-side paths.

fix: make builder language-agnostic and add build verification

- Added build verification: auto-detects npm/cargo/go build commands and runs them
- Non-source-only changes now retry instead of auto-passing
- Aider prompt is repo-aware instead of hardcoded to "Forge project"
- Removed --no-auto-commits: aider auto-commits, diffs against origin/main
- Smart reset: preserves aider's work on build failure, only resets on no-changes
- Auto dep install: parses aider output for npm/pip install commands and runs them
- Squash commit: all aider commits squashed into one clean commit before push

commit.message:
Replace Input.Password with CSS-masked text input to prevent browser password prompts

Browsers detect type="password" inputs regardless of autocomplete settings. Using a regular text input with CSS -webkit-text-security: disc provides the same visual masking without triggering browser password save prompts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
